title: A black bear is apparently making its way across southeastern Mass.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Black bear sightings reported in several southeastern Massachusetts towns, including Stoughton, Avon, and Randolph.
Residents advised to keep a safe distance if they encounter the bear and not to approach it.
Massachusetts Division of Fisheries and Wildlife provides guidelines for safely dealing with black bear encounters.

title: Mass. truck driver charged in killing of Penn. trooper
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Michael Bon, a Brockton truck driver, charged with homicide for a crash killing a Pennsylvania trooper on I-81.
The crash involved Bon's truck striking another truck and Trooper Pahira, who died from his injuries.
Bon is held on $700,000 bail; scheduled for a preliminary hearing on July 16 in Schuylkill County.

title: Shootings leave 2 dead, another 11 injured in Boston as councilors call for ‘public safety summit’
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Thirteen people were shot across five incidents in Boston on Saturday night, leaving two dead.
Boston City Councilors are calling for a public safety summit in response to the shootings.
Councilors demand increased police staffing and stricter measures against illegal fireworks usage in the city.

title: What Boston meteorologists are saying about the potential for heavy rain and flooding ahead
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Boston meteorologists predict heavy rain and potential flash flooding for the start of the week.
National Weather Service forecasts 1-3 inches of rain with possible localized bands of 4-6 inches.
A Flood Watch is in effect for southern New England, affecting parts of Massachusetts, Connecticut, and Rhode Island.

title: Hampton Beach shooting suspect dead; two injured
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A shooting at Hampton Beach, N.H. on Sunday injured two people and resulted in a suspect's death.
The suspect, Tyshawn Cooper, 21, of South Carolina, died from a self-inflicted gunshot wound.
The New Hampshire Attorney General's office is investigating the incident at 29 Ocean Blvd. in Hampton.

title: Across the Great Divide: Every Noah Kahan song ranked from worst to best
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Vermont singer-songwriter Noah Kahan releases fourth studio album and stars in a Netflix documentary in 2026.
Kahan's summer shows include performances at Fenway Park on July 7, 8, 10, and 11, 2026.
Boston.com ranks all 76 officially released Noah Kahan songs from worst to best, excluding remixes and collaborations.

title: Historic island house on 25 acres in Maine hits market
author: Regina Cole
bullets: Historic Gilbert Head in Georgetown, Maine, listed for $3.85 million, includes a Federal-style house and artist studio.
The property features a 3,346-square-foot main house, private dock, boathouse, and 25 acres of island land.
Gilbert Head is accessible only by boat and includes modern amenities with historic elements, sold fully furnished.

title: Northeastern University president’s compensation exceeds $3 million, records show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Joseph Aoun, Northeastern University president, earned over $3 million in total compensation for Fiscal Year 2025.
Aoun's compensation included $700,000 in deferred compensation and $550,000 in bonus and incentive pay.
Aoun's earnings surpassed other Massachusetts college leaders, with MIT's Sally Kornbluth earning $2.15 million.

title: Cambridge man killed in early-morning shooting, investigators say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, was found dead from a gunshot wound near Broadway and Norfolk Street in Cambridge.
The fatal shooting occurred shortly before 4:30 a.m., and was reported to authorities around 5:30 a.m.
Cambridge police and state detectives are investigating; information can be reported to police anonymously.

title: New England Aquarium trainer joins effort to save endangered penguin chicks in South Africa
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: New England Aquarium trainer Diana Major volunteered in South Africa to help save endangered African penguin chicks.
African penguins are critically endangered, with a 97% population decline, threatened by food scarcity, habitat loss, and disease.
The Aquarium plans to continue annual support for penguin conservation in South Africa through trainers and funding.
